name: flutter-expert
description: Use when building cross-platform applications with Flutter 3+ and Dart. Invoke for widget development, Riverpod/Bloc state management, GoRouter navigation, platform-specific implementations, performance optimization.
capabilities: Implement Flutter widgets with const optimization and proper state management
Set up Riverpod providers, Notifiers, and AsyncNotifier patterns
Create Bloc and Cubit architectures for complex state flows
Configure GoRouter navigation with deep linking and auth guards
Optimize Flutter performance using DevTools profiling and const constructors
Structure Flutter projects with clean architecture principles
author: Jeffallan
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation/reference skill for Flutter development. Static scanner flagged 88 external_command patterns and 1 network pattern, but all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misidentified Dart code blocks and CLI command documentation examples as shell execution. No actual security risks exist. All content is legitimate Flutter/Dart documentation with no prompt injection attempts.
valueStatement: Building mobile applications across iOS, Android, and web requires deep expertise in Flutter, state management, and platform-specific implementations. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for Flutter 3+ development with Riverpod, Bloc, and GoRouter patterns.
limitations: Does not generate complete production apps from scratch
Does not provide backend API implementation
Does not handle platform-specific native code beyond guidance
Does not test or execute generated code

useCases.title: Mobile developer building new Flutter app
useCases.description: Get guidance on setting up project structure, choosing state management approach, and implementing core widgets following Flutter best practices.

useCases.title: Experienced Flutter engineer optimizing performance
useCases.description: Learn performance optimization techniques including const widgets, selective rebuilding, RepaintBoundary usage, and DevTools profiling.

useCases.title: Full-stack developer adding Flutter to stack
useCases.description: Understand Flutter patterns and architecture to integrate with existing backend services and implement cross-platform features.

promptTemplates.title: Create a new Flutter screen
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Flutter screen with Riverpod state management that displays a list of items from an API. Include error handling, loading states, and pull-to-refresh functionality.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a new feature screen with async data

promptTemplates.title: Implement authentication flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a Bloc-based authentication flow with login, logout, and protected routes using GoRouter with auth redirect. Include session management and error states.

promptTemplates.title: Optimize widget rebuilds
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this Flutter widget and optimize unnecessary rebuilds. Show how to use const constructors, RepaintBoundary, and selective provider watching with select().

promptTemplates.title: Set up project structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a clean Flutter project structure for a medium-sized app. Include feature-based directory organization, dependency injection setup, and routing configuration.

bestPractices: Use const constructors on all static widgets to minimize rebuild overhead
Choose Riverpod for simple state and Bloc for complex event-driven workflows
Profile with DevTools before optimizing - measure, do not guess
Separate UI and business logic using proper state management patterns
antiPatterns: Building widgets inside build() methods - extract to const constructors
Using setState for app-wide state - use Riverpod providers instead
Skipping keys on list items - causes performance and animation issues
Blocking UI thread with heavy computation - use compute() and isolates

faq.question: When should I use Riverpod vs Bloc?
faq.answer: Use Riverpod for simple to moderate state management needs, particularly with reactive patterns. Use Bloc when you need explicit event-driven state transitions, complex business logic, or highly testable flows. Riverpod is generally simpler to set up.

faq.question: How do I optimize Flutter app performance?
faq.answer: Use const constructors everywhere possible, implement keys on list items, use ref.watch with select() for selective rebuilding, wrap expensive widgets in RepaintBoundary, and use ListView.builder for long lists. Always profile with DevTools first.

faq.question: What is the recommended project structure?
faq.answer: Use feature-based organization with folders for features, core services, shared widgets, and DI. Separate models, providers, and widgets within each feature. Avoid god files and keep widgets small and focused.

faq.question: How do I implement deep linking in Flutter?
faq.answer: Use GoRouter with path parameters (e.g., /details/:id). Configure platform-specific intent filters in AndroidManifest.xml and Info.plist. Test with flutter run and use uni_links package for handling incoming links.

faq.question: How do I test Flutter widgets and state?
faq.answer: Use flutter_test for widget tests with pumpWidget and find.byType. Test providers by reading values directly. Test Blocs using bloc_test package with expect() assertions on states.

faq.question: What are const constructors and why use them?
faq.answer: Const constructors create compile-time constant widgets that Flutter can reuse across rebuilds. They prevent unnecessary widget recreation, reducing rebuild overhead and improving performance. Always use const on static widgets.
